preamble: The transformation of a voiced velar consonant /g/ into a voiced labiodental fricative /v/, typically through a labiovelar stage or spirantization.

examples.note: Historical spirantization in Central Gur: earlier voiced stops shifted to fricatives including labiodentalfricative [v]. Also documented as *ɓ > b/v in some Gur languages.
PIE voiced labiovelar aspirated stop becoming /f/ or /v/ in Latin.
Velar weakening to glide, sometimes leading to labialized forms in related dialects.

sources: Sihler, A. L. (1995). New Comparative Grammar of Greek and Latin.
